imperial.strategic_description: This civ has strong infantry for frontline pressure, with Champion serving as the backbone of siege-backed pushes. Strong infantry anti-cavalry with Halberdier to hold defensive positions against cavalry pushes. Vulnerable to massed archers -- consider aggressive play before ranged compositions develop. Siege options are limited -- look to win through open-field engagements rather than pushing fortifications.

This civ has strong infantry for frontline pressure, with Elite Jaguar Warrior serving as the backbone of siege-backed pushes. Limited counter options mean this civ must play aggressively and press its advantage before opponents can mass their army. An infantry ram push is the signature play -- use Elite Jaguar Warrior as a meatshield to protect rams pushing into enemy bases.

This civ is versatile, with strength across cavalry and ranged -- allowing flexible strategies that adapt to any opponent. Can answer enemy cavalry with mobile options like Elite Ratha (Melee), allowing counter-raids and flexible responses. Good tools against ranged units help shut down archer compositions. Siege options are limited -- look to win through open-field engagements rather than pushing fortifications.

This civ has strong ranged options for concentrated pushes, with Elite Camel Archer providing range advantage and sustained damage output. Can answer enemy cavalry with mobile options like Heavy Camel Rider, allowing counter-raids and flexible responses. Good tools against ranged units help shut down archer compositions. Bombard Cannons provide long-range siege power for breaking through fortified positions.

This civ has strong ranged options for concentrated pushes, with Elite Hussite Wagon providing range advantage and sustained damage output. Strong infantry anti-cavalry with Halberdier to hold defensive positions against cavalry pushes. Good tools against ranged units help shut down archer compositions. Bombard Cannons provide long-range siege power for breaking through fortified positions.

This civ is versatile, with strength across cavalry and ranged -- allowing flexible strategies that adapt to any opponent. Excellent anti-cavalry options from both stable and barracks -- very hard for cavalry-heavy opponents to find an opening. Good tools against ranged units help shut down archer compositions. Solid siege options give flexibility in how to close out games.
